page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is configured with 128 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU physics tests ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks also reaches top-tier levels at 8132.17 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write bandwidth remain strong in the top 25%. In application-specific tests, the instance excels at ray tracing, software compilation with Clang, and Redis SET operations, which achieve 120.13 million operations per second.

Despite its massive parallel compute capacity, single-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded compression tasks landing in the middle 50%. The 1.0 GB of RAM per core represents a resource density tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases but highly cost-efficient for compute-bound parallel processing. Large LLM inference text generation is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% for 70B models due to the lack of hardware acceleration. This instance is best suited for highly parallelized workloads such as ray tracing, software compilation, static web serving, and high-throughput Redis deployments.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor and 128 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU acceleration, this server is designed for high-density parallel compute tasks. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Redis SET operations ranking in the top 10%. It also delivers strong memory bandwidth and software compilation speeds. However, the 1.0 GB of RAM per core represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications, and single-core performance remains average. This instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound, parallelized workloads such as ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and distributed compilation, rather than memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM. Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13, it delivers top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 features 128 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, offering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core compute instance designed for parallel processing, software compilation, and high-throughput distributed workloads without GPU requirements.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
128 GB of RAM providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU and Redis SET performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimal for parallel workloads, ray tracing, and software compilation
